story_title: Israel's 'buffer zone' and the redrawing of southern Lebanon
story_one_liner: While the world watches Hormuz, Israel is permanently changing the map of southern Lebanon. Four-hundred-thirty thousand displaced. Pope Leo XIV is calling priests on video. Four very different countries are telling four very different stories.
music_brief: subtle drone, mournful undertones, no sting cues
voiceover_provider: ElevenLabs Creator tier or Azure Neural TTS

scenes.voiceover: While everyone watches Hormuz, Israel is redrawing the map of southern Lebanon. Quietly.
scenes.visual_brief: Black screen, white text. Cut to satellite shot of Litani River area, evacuation orders highlighted in red.

scenes.voiceover: Israeli Defense Forces have established what they call a 'security zone'. Eighty towns evacuated. Four-hundred-thirty thousand Lebanese forced from their homes. Three-hundred-fifty-thousand more on top of those who never returned from earlier rounds.
scenes.visual_brief: Animated map zooming on southern Lebanon. Towns light up red as evacuation orders are issued. Population counter ticks up to 350,000.

scenes.voiceover: Saudi Arabia's Arab News uses scare quotes. Quote: "How Israel's 'buffer zone' plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon... orders to vacate have reached Beirut's southern suburbs, where residents have been told to leave their homes and not expect to return until Israel decides the time is right."
scenes.visual_brief: Saudi flag overlay. Headline card from Arab News with the scare quotes around 'buffer zone' highlighted. Cut to satellite view of Beirut.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🇸🇦 SAUDI: 'Until Israel decides the time is right'
Arab News
scenes.headline_quoted: How Israel's 'buffer zone' plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon — Arab News

scenes.voiceover: Egypt's Mada Masr is more direct. The headline doesn't say 'security zone.' It says: "Israel attacks villages north of the Litani to ease pressure on troops occupying crucial axis." One word: occupying.
scenes.visual_brief: Egyptian flag overlay. Mada Masr headline displayed with 'occupying' word visually emphasized. Cut to Lebanese village footage (from Mada Masr's source).
scenes.headline_quoted: Israel attacks villages north of Litani to ease pressure on troops occupying crucial axis — Mada Masr

scenes.voiceover: The Vatican stepped in. Pope Leo the Fourteenth video-called priests in the border villages personally. Quote: "the villages of the priests are along the border with Israel and are being worn down by missiles and bombardments."
scenes.visual_brief: Vatican flag overlay. Cut to footage of Pope Leo XIV. Phone-screen mockup of the video call with multiple priests' faces.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🇻🇦 VATICAN: 'Worn down by missiles'
Pope Leo XIV
scenes.headline_quoted: Pope Leo XIV video-calls priests in southern Lebanon — Vatican News

scenes.voiceover: Pan-Arab Middle East Eye surfaced the line nobody else ran. Israel's army chief calls this — quote — "a historic opportunity to reshape the region."
scenes.visual_brief: Map showing the broader Middle East. Quote card with Middle East Eye styling. Quote highlighted.
scenes.on_screen_text:
Pan-Arab MEE: 'Historic opportunity to reshape the region'
Israeli army chief, via Middle East Eye
scenes.headline_quoted: Israel army chief sees 'historic opportunity' to reshape region — Middle East Eye

scenes.voiceover: Channel News Asia. Same story. Wire framing. "Four killed despite ceasefire." Including the mayor of Zellaya and three of his family.
scenes.visual_brief: Singapore-Asia perspective overlay. Brief headline display, names of dead listed.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🌏 SE ASIA WIRE: 'Four killed despite ceasefire'
Channel News Asia
scenes.headline_quoted: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 4 despite ceasefire — Channel News Asia

scenes.voiceover: Same campaign. Five framings. While the world looks elsewhere.
scenes.visual_brief: Five overlay flags fade in around the southern Lebanon map. End card with channel handle.
scenes.on_screen_text:
Same campaign. Five framings.
Daily framings. Follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Lebanese Health Ministry + Israeli Defense Forces statements + on-the-ground reporting from Mada Masr, Middle East Eye, Channel News Asia

supporting_quotes_per_frame.exact_headline: How Israel's 'buffer zone' plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon
Israel attacks villages north of Litani to ease pressure on troops occupying crucial axis
Pope Leo XIV video-calls priests in southern Lebanon
Israel army chief sees 'historic opportunity' to reshape region
Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 4 despite ceasefire

fact_check_provenance.note: Lower bucket count (7) but highest framing-divergence of the day. Western mainstream press largely silent — itself part of the framing story (worth flagging in newsletter, not the video itself).
